video_subject: Forex Market: Dollar Strength Amid Fed Pressure
video_script: The US dollar just hit its strongest level since mid-May 2025, and the euro is feeling the pressure. EUR/USD slipped below 1.15 today, its lowest since late March, after the Federal Reserve held rates steady but flagged a possible hike. Here is the tension driving the market: Trump wants a weaker dollar to boost exports, but tariffs push it higher. The European Central Bank already raised rates last week, and the Bank of Japan followed days later. Traders are still leaning on the dollar as a safe haven. Now all eyes turn to the Federal Reserve and its new chair. Analysts see the euro drifting lower into year-end. For the forex market, one thing is clear: the next big move comes down to Washington.
